moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: polite-dormitory
logs.text:
tl;dr 

I believe that climate change should (EDIT: not) be used as an argument, since in order to be consistent, you must not use 90% of the products the western civilization manufactures (which I would bet you do, as all of us?). 

---------------------------------------

> climate change

/u/Iswallowedafly made a similar argument. 

Do you use a car? A fridge? Do you have more than two pair of shoes? Glasses? If you are a woman, do you dye your hair? If you are a man, do you use aftershave? Do you have more than one phone or a phone and a tablet and a kindle? 

None of the above are necessary since thousands of generations survived without them and none of them improves of life expectancy or health, yet all of them - either by using or by manufacturing  and bying them - cause climate change. As for the fact that these things do not cause pain or distress: most of these products are produced in asia and northern africa, taking advantage of children, while keeping safety measures to a minimum, leading to collapses of buildings like multiple times have happened in India, fires like it happened in Egypt etc.  

ps. For the fridge, you could go and buy every day what you need for that day - if you go walking then you add nothing that could be harmful to the environment.

logs.name: inscribed-station
logs.text:
I use some of those things. I don't justify any of their environmental impact by saying "well I also have a fridge so why does it even matter..." though.  

This isn't about being "consistent". This is about the largest contributor to the destruction of our planet. Which also happens to cause suffering and death to 50 billion sentient creatures (many as intelligent as children) annually. 

Those things you mentioned which have an environmental impact can have their necessity for modern society argued very easily. 

Please explain to me how eating meat is as necessary as wearing glasses? Please tell me how much less of an environmental impact we'd have if we couldn't REFRIGERATE food? 

Ignoring the incredible importance of refrigeration in the food supply chain, many would be unable to live their regular lives without refrigeration. So much lost time and productivity for how much of any reduced impact? What a nightmare that would be too organize too. 

And that's before you get to issues of people who live far from the store? Sure they could move and I'd even listen if you were actually making arguments against living in far away rural areas at a cost to the rest of society but I assume you're not. I personally live in a city and don't drive. 

Meanwhile not only are the advantages of abolishing animal ag extremely clear and infinitely more feasible, they would require no large changes to the lives of most, nor have a negative impact on society and require fundamental changes like other things you mentioned. 

All while having a more positive impact than all of those things put together. 

What's the environmental impact of hair dye?

Please tell me how you'd support everyone's right to buy hairdye if hairdye were the single largest contributor to global warming. More than everything else combined. 

It's sad that "you're not perfect" is considered a fair argument to "meat is immoral and unnecessary lets stop". Again, my views on the morality of meat are not a personal attack on you. This is a debate on what we do as a society.

And do you have any suggestions for how someone can cut their impact from all the things you mentioned above without significant impacts on their life? What do you do to help?

logs.name: abstract-water
logs.text: Wow, this is a really frustrating argument. It feels like you're trying to deflect from the core issue by cherry-picking examples instead of actually addressing the scale of the problem. It's not about being "perfect," it's about acknowledging the massive impact of our lifestyles. And conveniently ignoring the fact that a lot of these products are made under incredibly unethical conditions. @polite-dorm you missed the point entirely.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: @polite-dorm, @inscribed-station, @abstract-water, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic.

logs.name: inscribed-station
logs.text: Seriously?! Cherry-picking examples doesn't deflect from the point, it makes it worse. And conveniently ignoring unethical labor practices is just plain obtuse. @abstract-water you missed the entire point.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@abstract-water, @inscribed-station, let's take a step back and refocus on the topic at hand. We're discussing the environmental impact of consumer choices, not labor practices. @inscribed-station, I'd appreciate it if you could refrain from making assumptions about others' intentions. Let's keep the conversation constructive."

logs.name: shy-swallow
logs.text: Oh dear goodness, honestly! That's just not helpful, is it? Seems like you're trying to shift the conversation onto something completely different. I guess it's always easier to point fingers than to actually address the core issue. @polite-dorm, I think a little less nitpicking and a little more focus would be appreciated.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation on track. @shy-swallow, I appreciate your input, but I'd like to clarify that my previous response was an attempt to refocus the discussion, not shift it. @inscribed-station, I understand your concerns, but let's discuss them in the context of the topic at hand. @shy-swallow, I'm not nitpicking, I'm trying to maintain a productive conversation. Let's keep it civil and on-topic."

logs.name: future-billet
logs.text: Well bless my heart. Seems folks are determined to muddy the waters. Trying to keep things focused is one thing, but constantly calling people out for "nitpicking" is just plain rude. Back to the point, I suppose.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation productive, folks. @future-billet, I appreciate your input, but let's not make assumptions about intentions. If you feel someone is being rude, please point it out specifically. @shy-swallow, @inscribed-station, let's keep the discussion civil and on-topic. No further warnings will be issued at this time."

logs.name: dry-wrestler
logs.text: Oh honey, bless your heart too. Keeping things "on track" sounds awfully close to policing opinions to me. Seems like a whole lot of folks are trying to control what people say. I'm just trying to have a decent conversation without being constantly pointed out for "nitpicking." It's exhausting!

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation going, folks. @dry-wrestler, I understand your concerns, but let's not conflate keeping the discussion on-topic with policing opinions. We're here to facilitate a respectful exchange of ideas, not stifle them. If you feel like your contributions are being dismissed or undervalued, please don't hesitate to speak up. @future-billet, @shy-swallow, @inscribed-station, let's keep the discussion civil and focused. No further warnings will be issued at this time."

logs.name: polite-dormitory
logs.text: Ugh, seriously? That's kinda sus. Feels like being told what to say. I guess I'll just try to be nice lol.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation respectful, @polite-dormitory. No need to be sarcastic. If you have concerns, feel free to express them, but let's avoid passive-aggressive comments."

logs.name: each-landfall
logs.text: Seriously?! That's so unnecessary. I guess I'll just try to be nice then lol. It feels like I'm being watched.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: @each-landfall, let's keep the conversation civil.

logs.name: dry-wrestler
logs.text: Oh honey, bless your heart. You folks need to learn some basic manners. Seriously, it's not rocket science to not repeat yourself! I'm trying to help people here, and it's getting old. @future-billet, I appreciate you trying to contribute, but some of us are trying to be constructive too. And @inscribed-station, really? Two identical posts? You're giving me a headache.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: @future-billet, @dry-wrestler, let's keep the discussion respectful. @dry-wrestler, while I appreciate your passion, refrain from using colloquial expressions like "honey" and "bless your heart" in a condescending manner.

logs.text: Ugh, seriously? Some people are just trying way too hard to be mean. I get that you're trying to help, but calling people names isn't cool @dry-wrestler. Can we just chill?

logs.name: moderator
